series.title: hw/riscv/virt-acpi-build.c: Use kvm timer frequency when kvm enabled

review.summary: The change is small and matches existing RISC-V virt behavior for DT guests: RHCT should advertise the host-provided timebase when KVM is active instead of the fixed ACLINT default. I did not identify a functional issue in the patch itself. Residual risk remains because checkpatch, git status inspection, and build/test validation were not run.

A.details: Problem statement and fix direction are coherent. ACPI RHCT time base frequency should reflect the effective timer frequency seen by a KVM guest, not the TCG/default ACLINT constant.

B.details: The code updates only the RHCT time base field and gates the KVM-specific path behind kvm_enabled(). That is the expected behavior change and is consistent with the commit message.

C.details: The added includes and helper usage appear appropriate for a RISC-V KVM-specific query path. No obvious build-configuration issue is visible from the patch alone, though this was not compile-verified.

D.details: Regression risk is low because non-KVM behavior remains unchanged and the KVM path is limited to the advertised RHCT frequency value.

E.details: Recommendation is to take the patch, with the usual caveat that a build and a basic KVM boot validation should still be performed.

disclaimer: LLM-generated draft. Not an authoritative review.
